from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects about universities (or related entities)
    into a single fluent, complex sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Build a mapping from subject to its triples
    subject_map = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_map:
            subject_map[t.subject] = []
        subject_map[t.subject].append(t)

    # Identify the main subject (typically a university/institution)
    main_subject = None
    for subj in subject_map:
        if "University" in subj or "College" in subj or "Institute" in subj or "School" in subj:
            main_subject = subj
            break

    if main_subject is None and subject_map:
        main_subject = list(subject_map.keys())[0]

    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase template.
    templates = {
        "city": "located in {object}",
        "country": "in {object}",
        "latinName": "Latin name is {object}",
        "nickname": "nickname is {object}",
        "rector": "rector is {object}",
        "academicStaffSize": "academic staff of {object}",
        "established": "established in {object}",
        "state": "in the state of {object}",
        "governmentType": "government type is {object}",
        "hasToItsNortheast": "has {object} to its northeast",
        "dean": "dean is {object}",
        "location": "located in {object}",
        "numberOfStudents": "{object} students",
        "affiliation": "affiliated with {object}",
        "director": "director is {object}",
        "motto": "motto is \"{object}\"",
        "numberOfPostgraduateStudents": "{object} postgraduate students",
        "numberOfUndergraduateStudents": "{object} undergraduate students",
        "officialSchoolColour": "official school colour is {object}",
        "outlookRanking": "ranked {object} in the Outlook ranking",
        "sportsOffered": "offers {object}",
        "wasGivenTheTechnicalCampusStatusBy": "given technical campus status by {object}",
        "founder": "founded by {object}",
        "leader": "led by {object}",
        "leaderTitle": "leader title is {object}",
        "religion": "religion is {object}",
        "headquarter": "headquarter is {object}",
        "largestCity": "largest city is {object}",
        "river": "river is {object}",
        "hasToItsWest": "has {object} to its west",
        "anthem": "anthem is \"{object}\"",
        "ethnicGroup": "ethnic group includes {object}",
        "legislature": "legislature is {object}",
        "sportGoverningBody": "sport governing body is {object}",
        "isPartOf": "part of {object}",
        "neighboringMunicipality": "neighboring municipality is {object}",
        "capital": "capital is {object}",
        "patronSaint": "patron saint is {object}",
        "hasToItsNorthwest": "has {object} to its northwest",
        "campus": "campus is located at {object}",
        "president": "president is {object}",
        "staff": "{object} staff members",
        "numberOfDoctoralStudents": "{object} doctoral students",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "elevation of {object} meters above sea level",
        "postalCode": "postal code is {object}",
        "longName": "long name is {object}",
    }

    # Build the sentence by organizing triples into main and related entity clauses
    main_triples = subject_map.get(main_subject, [])
    related_entities = {}  # Maps entity name to its triples

    # Find related entities (objects that are also subjects)
    for t in main_triples:
        if t.object in subject_map and t.object != main_subject:
            related_entities[t.object] = subject_map[t.object]

    # Build main clause with all main subject predicates
    main_clauses = []
    for t in main_triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate, "{predicate} {object}")
        clause = tmpl.format(object=t.object)
        main_clauses.append(clause)

    # Build related entity clauses
    related_clauses = []
    for entity, entity_triples in related_entities.items():
        entity_clauses = []
        for t in entity_triples:
            tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate, "{predicate} {object}")
            clause = tmpl.format(object=t.object)
            entity_clauses.append(clause)
        related_clauses.append((entity, entity_clauses))

    # Combine all into a single fluent sentence
    result_parts = [f"{main_subject} {main_clauses[0]}"]
    for clause in main_clauses[1:]:
        result_parts.append(", and " + clause)

    for entity, entity_clauses in related_clauses:
        result_parts.append(f", which {entity} {entity_clauses[0]}")
        for clause in entity_clauses[1:]:
            result_parts.append(", and " + clause)

    sentence = "".join(result_parts) + "."
    return sentence if sentence else ""

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
